headline: Virtual Reality for the 286 Processor
lede: A deep dive into the limitations of 1980s silicon reveals how modern ingenuity can conjure immersive dreamscapes on legacy hardware. This experiment proves that even the most constrained clock cycles can support surprising visual depth.

headline: Linking AI Conversations to Repository Commits
lede: As machine-generated code becomes the industry standard, the lack of context in our version history poses a significant transparency risk. Bridging the gap between intent and implementation requires treating the AI session as a first-class citizen in the git ecosystem.
pullquote: The rationale behind a model's output is as vital to the codebase as the generated logic itself

headline: WebMCP Framework Enters Early Preview
lede: Chrome developers introduce a bridge for the Model Context Protocol, aimed at standardizing how browsers interact with local and remote AI models. This release provides a unified interface for developers building agentic workflows directly in the web environment.

headline: Public Records Ruling Forces Flock Network Closure
lede: Everett officials have shuttered their private surveillance camera network following a judge's determination that the captured footage constitutes a public record. The decision marks a significant boundary in the ongoing debate over private-public infrastructure partnerships.

headline: Compiling Classical Models Into Native C99 Code
lede: Timber strips away the Python runtime overhead to deliver high-performance inference for classical machine learning tasks. By bypassing typical dependency chains, the engine achieves a massive leap in processing speed.

headline: Right-Sizing Large Language Models for Local Hardware
lede: Query your local system architecture to match models with available VRAM and CPU threads. This utility streamlines deployment by automating the complex process of hardware resource verification.

headline: A Zero-Browser Typesetting Engine for Bit-Perfect PDFs
lede: By ignoring the DOM in favor of a coordinate-based simulation, this engine renders documents with absolute precision. The architecture relies on pure TypeScript to ensure high-performance output across isolated server-side environments.

headline: Frankensqlite Brings Concurrency to Rust
lede: This re-implementation explores the limits of SQLite by introducing a novel approach to handle multiple concurrent writers. It dissects the classic ACID model to rebuild data persistence for the modern multicore era.

headline: Ghostty Terminal Emulator
lede: A performance-focused terminal emulator designed for high-speed input and seamless integration. The interface balances technical depth with a refined approach to modern console management.

headline: Revisiting Tove Jansson’s Hobbit Illustrations
lede: G Studies of Jansson’s visual interpretation of Middle-earth provide a unique perspective on the intersection of Nordic folklore and canonical fantasy. These illustrations, once met with critical skepticism, are now viewed as a masterclass in atmospheric rendering.
